title: rhododendron dauricum subsp. mucronulatum
intro: Rhododendron dauricum subsp. mucronulatum, also known as Korean rhododendron, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its showy white to pale pink flowers and leathery leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to help alleviate various health issues. The health benefits of Rhododendron dauricum subsp. mucronulatum include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to prevent the growth of certain bacteria and viruses. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its flowers and leaves in hot water to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
